verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass TM protein resident in the Golgi where it glycosylates proteins in transit. However, it has a well-documented dual localization: a significant fraction is shed as a soluble form and, importantly, a distinct surface form is displayed on the plasma membrane of many cell types (detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry). The surface pool is not constitutive in all cell types and co-mingles with the dominant Golgi-resident pool, making this a dual_localization contextual call rather than a straightforward yes.
